text:

    "Brent crude at $118.95/barrel (Apr 29, 2026), up ~86% YoY from ~$64, driven by Hormuz disruption blocking ~20% of global oil trade — direct input-cost and fiscal pressure on India's infrastructure and capital-goods sector, the precise thematic exposure of Ambit Build India Portfolio",
    "INR at 94.79 vs USD (Apr 29, 2026), a 10.7% depreciation over 12 months from 85.60 (Apr 2, 2025); forex reserves down $25B to $703B — imported inflation risk and sovereign balance-sheet pressure rising",
    "FII net equity outflows of Rs 56,401 Cr in April 2026 alone (~$5.97B), with heaviest single-day outflow of Rs 19,837 Cr on Apr 2; DII absorption only partial (Rs 34,617 Cr through Apr 8) — equity market liquidity and valuation support under stress",
    "RBI repo at 5.25% (hold, Apr 17, 2026) after 125 bps of cuts; real rate derived at ~3.15% (repo minus core CPI of 2.1%) — real rates are materially restrictive, compressing equity risk-premium headroom and raising the hurdle rate for capital-goods project economics",
    "10Y government bond yield at 6.99% (Apr 17, 2026), up 44 bps in five months, spiking to 7.19% on Apr 3 before partial retracement — rising long-end yields increase discount rates for long-duration infrastructure cash flows, directly pressuring infrastructure-thematic equity valuations",
    "GDP growth strong (Q3 FY26: 7.8% YoY; manufacturing 13.3%) and government capex robust (Rs 9.29L Cr YTD, +14.46% YoY, 84.81% of RE) — these are structural positives for the infrastructure/capital-goods thematic over a multi-year horizon",
    "PMI Manufacturing rebounding to 55.9 (Apr flash) after Mar dip to 53.9; capacity utilisation at 75.6% (Q3 FY26, above 75% late-expansion threshold) — domestic industrial cycle remains constructive, supporting the thematic rationale",
    "IMD forecast of below-normal monsoon (92% of LPA, 66% probability of below-normal or deficient outcome) — upside risk to food inflation, which could constrain RBI's ability to cut further and sustain fiscal transfer capacity",
    "US Fed holding at 3.50–3.75% (Mar 18, 2026 FOMC); India-US rate differential at 162 bps — adequate but not compelling for carry-driven FII re-entry; leadership transition at Fed (Warsh ~May 15) adds near-term policy uncertainty",
    "Government fiscal consolidation on track (FY26 YTD deficit -7.0% YoY; FY27 target 4.3% of GDP) with continued capex push (FY27 effective capex Rs 17.15L Cr) — medium-term infrastructure order-book visibility remains intact"

  "reasoning_paragraph": "The macro environment as of the case decision date (2026-04-02, with the most current data points extending to Apr 29, 2026) presents a structurally bifurcated picture for the proposed Ambit Build India Portfolio commitment: the domestic real-economy and fiscal backdrop remains constructive for an infrastructure/capital-goods thematic, but the external and financial-conditions environment has deteriorated materially and introduces elevated near-term risk precisely at the point of entry. On the constructive side, Q3 FY26 GDP growth of 7.8% with manufacturing at 13.3%, government capex running at Rs 9.29L Cr YTD (+14.46% YoY, 84.81% of revised estimate), PMI Manufacturing rebounding to 55.9 (Apr flash), and capacity utilisation crossing 75.6% collectively affirm that the domestic industrial cycle underpinning the thematic is intact. Against this, three concurrent external shocks materially alter the risk-adjusted entry calculus: Brent crude has risen ~86% YoY to $118.95/barrel on Hormuz disruption, directly pressuring input costs, project economics, and the fiscal transfer capacity that funds infrastructure capex; the INR has depreciated 10.7% over twelve months to 94.79, amplifying imported inflation and eroding real returns for a rupee-denominated portfolio; and FII equity outflows of Rs 56,401 Cr in April alone signal that global risk-off is actively repricing Indian equities, with DII absorption only partial. Compounding these, the 10Y yield has risen 44 bps to 6.99% (spiking to 7.19% on Apr 3), and the derived real policy rate of ~3.15% is materially restrictive — both factors compress the valuation headroom for long-duration infrastructure cash flows. The RBI's neutral stance and unanimous hold at 5.25% reflect the geopolitical uncertainty the MPC itself has cited, and the IMD's below-normal monsoon forecast (66% probability) introduces a further upside risk to food inflation that could constrain the rate-cut path. None of these factors invalidate the multi-year thematic thesis for infrastructure and capital goods, but they collectively indicate that the current entry window carries elevated macro-level risk, particularly given that the proposed Rs 2 Cr commitment arrives with the portfolio already in equity-band breach (72.2% vs. a 70% ceiling) and would push PMS wrapper concentration from approximately 39% to 45% of advisory liquid AUM.",

    "CPI headline: 3.40% (Mar 2026); core CPI (ex-food, fuel): 2.1% (Jan–Feb avg)",
    "CPI food: 3.87% (Mar 2026)",
    "IMD monsoon forecast: 92% of LPA, below normal; P(below normal + deficient) = 66% (Apr 13, 2026)",
    "US Fed funds rate: 3.50–3.75% (hold, Mar 18, 2026 FOMC); Warsh succession ~May 15",
